message:
🐛 Fix Copilot trigger to use @copilot mention instead of assignment

The previous approach tried to assign copilot-swe-agent[bot] as an issue
assignee, but that doesn't work. Copilot coding agent is triggered by
mentioning @copilot in a comment with instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -73,41 +73,46 @@ jobs:
           gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --remove-label "ai-fix-requested" 2>/dev/null || true
           gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --add-label "ai-processing"
 
-      - name: Assign Copilot to issue
+      - name: Trigger Copilot via comment
         id: assign_copilot
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
-          REPO_OWNER="${{ github.repository_owner }}"
-          REPO_NAME="${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
           ISSUE_NUM="${{ steps.issue.outputs.number }}"
+          ISSUE_TITLE="${{ steps.issue_details.outputs.title }}"
 
-          # Get issue node ID
-          ISSUE_ID=$(gh api graphql -f query="
-            query {
-              repository(owner: \"$REPO_OWNER\", name: \"$REPO_NAME\") {
-                issue(number: $ISSUE_NUM) {
-                  id
-                }
-              }
-            }" --jq '.data.repository.issue.id')
+          echo "Triggering Copilot for issue #$ISSUE_NUM..."
 
-          echo "Issue ID: $ISSUE_ID"
+          # Mention @copilot in a comment to start a coding session
+          # Copilot coding agent responds to @copilot mentions with instructions
+          COMMENT_RESULT=$(gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUM" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" --body "$(cat <<EOF
+          ## 🤖 AI Fix Requested
 
-          # Assign Copilot to the issue using the REST API
-          # The assignee must be "copilot-swe-agent[bot]" (with [bot] suffix)
-          echo "Assigning Copilot to issue #$ISSUE_NUM..."
+          @copilot Please analyze this issue and create a fix.
 
-          ASSIGN_RESULT=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$ISSUE_NUM/assignees \
-            -X POST \
-            --input - <<< '{"assignees": ["copilot-swe-agent[bot]"]}' 2>&1) || true
+          ### Instructions:
+          1. Read the issue description carefully
+          2. Explore the codebase to understand the context
+          3. Implement the requested changes
+          4. Create a PR with \`Fixes #$ISSUE_NUM\` in the description
+          5. Ensure the code follows existing patterns and style
+
+          ### Guidelines:
+          - Follow the patterns in existing code
+          - Add appropriate tests if applicable
+          - Keep changes focused on the issue scope
+          - Use TypeScript for frontend code
+          - Use Go for backend code
 
-          # Check if Copilot was successfully assigned
-          if echo "$ASSIGN_RESULT" | grep -q '"login":"Copilot"'; then
-            echo "Copilot successfully assigned to issue #$ISSUE_NUM"
+          Please start working on this issue.
+          EOF
+          )" 2>&1) && COMMENT_SUCCESS=true || COMMENT_SUCCESS=false
+
+          if [ "$COMMENT_SUCCESS" = "true" ]; then
+            echo "Successfully posted Copilot trigger comment on issue #$ISSUE_NUM"
             echo "copilot_assigned=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           else
-            echo "::warning::Failed to assign Copilot. Response: $ASSIGN_RESULT"
+            echo "::warning::Failed to post comment. Response: $COMMENT_RESULT"
             echo "copilot_assigned=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           fi
 
@@ -126,7 +131,7 @@ jobs:
           gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --remove-label "ai-processing" 2>/dev/null || true
           gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --add-label "ai-awaiting-fix"
 
-      - name: Handle Copilot assignment failed
+      - name: Handle Copilot trigger failed
         if: steps.assign_copilot.outputs.copilot_assigned != 'true'
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
@@ -143,17 +148,13 @@ jobs:
 
           # Add comment explaining the situation
           gh issue comment "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
-          ⚠️ **Copilot SWE Agent Unavailable**
-
-          The AI fix workflow was triggered, but GitHub Copilot SWE Agent is not available for this repository.
+          ⚠️ **Copilot Trigger Failed**
 
-          **Possible reasons:**
-          - Copilot Workspace may not be enabled for this repository
-          - The repository may not have access to Copilot SWE Agent features
+          The AI fix workflow was triggered, but failed to post the Copilot trigger comment.
 
           **Next steps:**
-          - A maintainer can manually address this issue
-          - Or enable Copilot Workspace for this repository and re-run the workflow
+          - A maintainer can manually trigger Copilot by commenting `@copilot` on this issue
+          - Or manually address this issue
 
           /cc @${{ github.repository_owner }}
           EOF
